title: The Mother of All Economic Shocks Is Chinese Mercantilism
content: Because analysts tend to put the United States at the center of the global economic story, they have overlooked the single most important force of the past 50 years. But it will be obvious to later generations that Chinese mercantilism has been more globally consequential than any other economic shock or policy choice.

creator: Arvind Subramanian
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Letting Russia Burn
content: From relentless Ukrainian drone strikes to soaring inflation and fuel shortages, Russia is paying an ever-higher price for Vladimir Putin's war of choice. But the war has become the organizing principle of Putin’s regime, leaving no path to lasting peace without a fundamental political transformation.
snippet: From relentless Ukrainian drone strikes to soaring inflation and fuel shortages, Russia is paying an ever-higher price for Vladimir Putin's war of choice. But the war has become the organizing
creator: Nina L. Khrushcheva
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What AI Governance Demands
content: AI governance must account for everything from the technology’s material requirements to its effects on communities, businesses, labor markets, the environment, and strategic competition. To this end, a framework of verification-based trust—broader than national regulation and more flexible than a classic treaty—is essential.

creator: Ana Palacio
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Japanese Wake-Up Call for America
content: Japan’s deepening currency and bond-market woes should be a wake-up call for other countries that appear to be on unsustainable fiscal paths, not least the United States, as well as France, Italy, and the United Kingdom. After all, a crisis in one country often draws investors' attention to others facing similar problems.
snippet: Japan’s deepening currency and bond-market woes should be a wake-up call for other countries that appear to be on unsustainable fiscal paths, not least the United States, as well as France, Italy, and
creator: Desmond Lachman
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Israel-Lebanon Recipe for Never-Ending War
content: Lebanon’s political circumstances, together with Iran’s strengthened regional position, make Hezbollah’s complete disarmament highly unlikely. By conditioning Israel’s withdrawal from Lebanon on this outcome—but with no provisions about cutting Hezbollah’s Iranian lifeline—the new framework agreement is dead on arrival.

creator: Shlomo Ben-Ami
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why This Energy Shock Is Different
content: Unlike recent energy crises, today’s geopolitical shock is destroying supply rather than rerouting it, exposing the limits of the standard policy toolkit. Governments can mitigate its impact on those most exposed, but only if fiscal and monetary policy work in concert.
snippet: Unlike recent energy crises, today’s geopolitical shock is destroying supply rather than rerouting it, exposing the limits of the standard policy toolkit. Governments can mitigate its impact on those
creator: Gene Frieda
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: China’s Erasure of Tibet Is Accelerating
content: In recent years, the international community has stood idly by while China has systematically eradicated Tibetan culture and identity. With the authorities stepping up their assimilation efforts, Asia risks not only losing one of its oldest civilizations, but also facing a China that is bigger, bolder, and more powerful than ever.

creator: Brahma Chellaney
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Judging the Enhanced Games
content: If fairness in sports required a level playing field that all who wish to compete could afford, that would be a reason for banning expensive performance-enhancing drugs. But it would be a reason for banning expensive high-tech swimming suits and expensive high-tech running shoes, too.

creator: Peter Singer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Is the Digital-Money Debate Really About?
content: Both the US and Europe are moving to create digital money, but neither has the right idea about how to do it. The key is to allow “narrow banks,” which issue money but not credit, to keep their reserves in central banks and provide capped interest payments to coin holders.

creator: Lucrezia Reichlin
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: An Athenian View of American Democracy
content: At 250, America is now producing a mindless, raw power and exponentially growing instability. The possibility of catharsis that characterized the United States at its bicentennial is hard to envisage a half-century later—to the detriment of us all.
snippet: At 250, America is now producing a mindless, raw power and exponentially growing instability. The possibility of catharsis that characterized the United States at its bicentennial is hard to envisage
creator: Yanis Varoufakis
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Low-Cost Solutions to Maternal Mortality Already Exist
content: As foreign aid declines, policymakers in lower-income countries must increasingly focus on making better use of scarce resources. When it comes to maternal mortality, that means investing in simple, proven, and affordable interventions for postpartum hemorrhage, pre-eclampsia, and obstructed labor.

creator: Ifeanyi M. Nsofor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Postmodern Economics
content: Eschewing the idea of a single truth, the recently deceased English artist David Hockney showed that the world unfolds before us in ways we cannot fully understand. After long clinging to the pretense of certainty, economists would do well to embrace the same insight.
snippet: Eschewing the idea of a single truth, the recently deceased English artist David Hockney showed that the world unfolds before us in ways we cannot fully understand. After long clinging to the pretense
creator: Angus Armstrong
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Digital Public Infrastructure Is Now a Strategic Battleground
content: Digital identity platforms, payment systems, and data exchanges promise more efficient, inclusive, and responsive public services. But in an era of geopolitical rivalry and concentrated digital power, those same systems create strategic dependencies that governments can no longer afford to ignore.
snippet: Digital identity platforms, payment systems, and data exchanges promise more efficient, inclusive, and responsive public services. But in an era of geopolitical rivalry and concentrated digital power,
creator: Tariq Malik
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Should the Bank of Japan Raise Interest Rates?
content: At the beginning of 2013, when "Abenomics" was rolled out in Japan, the yen was significantly overvalued, and looser monetary policy was badly needed. Today, the opposite is true, indicating that interest-rate hikes may well be the right prescription for Japan.

creator: Koichi Hamada
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Argentina Can Protect Its Resource Wealth
content: Alaska, Norway, and Chile recognized the economic and political risks that accompany commodity booms and established sovereign wealth funds to preserve their resource revenues. Argentina’s hydrocarbon- and lithium-rich provinces should learn from their experience.
snippet: Alaska, Norway, and Chile recognized the economic and political risks that accompany commodity booms and established sovereign wealth funds to preserve their resource revenues. Argentina’s
creator: Conrado Tenaglia
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Reinventing Development Finance
content: Faced with shrinking concessional finance and rising borrowing costs, Rwanda has worked with the World Bank Group to leverage limited public funds and risk-sharing instruments to unlock private capital at scale and on affordable terms. It's an arrangement holding important lessons for low-income countries that must do more with less.

creator: Yusuf Murangwa
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Mamdani’s American Dream
content: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s Independence Day address was a perfect example of ideology in the positive sense of the term. It overturned both the right-wing populist narrative of the United States and the left-wing "woke" critique with a vision that has the potential to mobilize a majority.

creator: Slavoj Žižek
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Vulnerable Are US Financial Markets?
content: Fueled by more than $1 trillion in borrowed money and rising leverage across the financial system, US stock markets may have entered bubble territory. Two developments could trigger a sharp correction: higher interest rates and a loss of confidence in the handful of tech giants driving the AI boom.

creator: Dambisa Moyo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Concessional Finance Is Needed More Than Ever
content: As debt burdens rise and climate shocks intensify, sustainable financing mechanisms are becoming indispensable. The Islamic Development Bank’s new concessional fund offers a promising model for mobilizing the resources needed to protect development gains and invest in resilience.
snippet: As debt burdens rise and climate shocks intensify, sustainable financing mechanisms are becoming indispensable. The Islamic Development Bank’s new concessional fund offers a promising model for
creator: Muhammad Al Jasser
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Purpose of Development Cooperation Is to Be Unnecessary
content: The original promise of development cooperation was to help countries overcome poverty, build productive economies, and reduce their dependence on external resources. That has not happened, and ongoing reviews of development cooperation, which focus on donor countries' needs and priorities, are unlikely to change this.

creator: Vitalice Meja
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Oil Shocks Are No Longer So Shocking
content: Despite this year's US/Israeli-Iran war causing the largest-ever disruption to global oil supplies, the shocks of the 1970s had a greater impact. Because oil has been used as a weapon for so long, markets, policymakers, and strategists have adapted.
